assumptions: The intragroup dividend has been declared and recognized as an intragroup receivable/payable, so it is a monetary item.
The entities involved have different functional currencies, so the related foreign exchange gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation and can affect consolidated profit or loss.
The general IFRS 9 hedge designation, documentation, and effectiveness requirements are assumed to be met for any hedge relationship considered.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognized intragroup dividend receivable/payable is a recognized asset or liability, so a fair value hedge is a directly relevant hedge accounting model to consider.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a separate hedge accounting model for risk on recognized assets or liabilities, making it a distinct treatment to consider for the foreign currency exposure.
